name: Muhammad ibn al-Abbas
title: Young Martyr
shortDescription: One of the young sons of Hazrat Abbas (AS) who was present at Karbala and was martyred.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn al-Abbas was another son of Hazrat Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas (AS) and Lubaba. According to some historical texts, he was a young teenager during the event of Karbala.

In addition to his loyalty and brotherhood, Hazrat Abbas (AS) had brought his family along on this perilous journey to sacrifice everything he had for Imam Hussain (AS). Muhammad was present in the tents alongside the other Hashemite children, witnessing the bitter scenes of Ashura.

After the martyrdom of his brave father at Alqamah, and with Yazid's army attacking the Ahl al-Bayt's camp on the afternoon of Ashura, the looting and burning of the tents began.

According to some narrations, during this brutal attack, Muhammad ibn al-Abbas was martyred unjustly and solitarily amidst the flames or under the trampling hooves of horses, his pure blood mixing with his father's in Karbala.
quoteArabic: وَقيلَ: لِلعَبّاسِ ابْنٌ يُقالُ لَهُ مُحَمَّدٌ قُتِلَ بِكَرْبَلاءَ...
quote: (In historical books:) It is said Abbas had a son named Muhammad who was killed in Karbala.
source: Manaqib Al Abi Talib, Ibn Shahr Ashub
